name: Baked Party Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cut a hole about 5-inch in diameter in the top of the bread (optional: make a decorative zigzag pattern). Reserve the crusty part for the loaf's top.
3. Scoop out most of the soft inside portion and set aside.
4. In a skillet, sauté green onions and garlic in butter until onions wilt. Do not burn!
5. Soften cream cheese and cut into small chunks. Place in a medium-sized bowl; add onions, garlic, sour cream, and shredded cheddar.
6. Mix well.
7. Drain non-marinated artichoke hearts and cut into quarters. Fold in the artichoke hearts.
8. Fill the hollowed-out bread with the mixture. Replace the top of bread and wrap tightly in a double thickness of heavy-duty aluminum foil.
9. Bake for about 1-1/2 hours.
10. Remove foil and top of bread; serve using thin slices of warm garlic bread as dippers.
Note: The best part of this dish is when the dip is gone, and all you have left is the bread soaked in delicious ingredients. Just break the bread up and pass it around!
